headline: pki.cmp.build gains the CA/responder side -- certificate, revocation, key-recovery, general, error, poll, and confirmation responses complete the RFC 9810 message surface.
summary: The CMP response side. pki.cmp.build's message.body now names a CA/responder arm as well as a request arm: ip / cp / kup / ccp carry a CertRepMessage (caPubs plus a response of CertResponse entries, each a PKIStatusInfo and, under a granting status, a certifiedKeyPair naming the issued certificate); rp a RevRepContent (revocation statuses, revoked CertIds, CRLs); krp a KeyRecRepContent (a recovered key-pair history); genp a general response; error an ErrorMsgContent (status, error code, details); pollRep a poll response (checkAfter delay); and pkiconf the final confirmation. The response arms reuse the request-side header, EXPLICIT-tag envelope, ProtectedPart, and signature or PBMAC1 protection wholesale, and are protected and self-verified the same way, so every response round-trips through pki.schema.cmp.parse. The RFC 9810 section 5.3.4 coherence rules are enforced -- a CertResponse carries a certifiedKeyPair only under a granting status and never alongside a failInfo, the certificate CHOICE alternative is a validated X.509 certificate, and a ccp response carries exactly one CertResponse.

Added: pki.cmp.build message.body now accepts the CA/responder-side arms: ip / cp / kup / ccp (a CertRepMessage -- caPubs plus a response of CertResponse entries, each carrying a PKIStatusInfo and, under a granting status, a certifiedKeyPair), rp (a RevRepContent), krp (a KeyRecRepContent), genp (a general response), error (an ErrorMsgContent), pollRep (a poll response), and pkiconf (the final confirmation). They reuse the request-side header, envelope, ProtectedPart, and signature / PBMAC1 protection, and round-trip through pki.schema.cmp.parse. The RFC 9810 section 5.3.4 rules are enforced (a certifiedKeyPair only under a granting status and never with a failInfo, a validated certificate CHOICE, a single-response ccp). The private-key-transport / KEM encrypted forms ride a pre-encoded escape hatch.
